# Colorado Income Tax — Agent Training Manual
## For Internal Use — Customer Service Representatives

This manual covers the most common Colorado individual income tax questions you will receive. Use it to provide accurate answers to callers. If a situation falls outside these guidelines, escalate to a senior agent.

---

## Who Needs to File?

Colorado requires a state return from residents and nonresidents with Colorado-source income when gross income exceeds:

- **Single filers: $13,590** *(Note: verify this figure with current policy — this was the 2024 threshold)*
- Married filing jointly: $29,200
- Head of household: $21,900

Tell callers: "If you're a single filer and earned more than $13,590 from any source, you'll need to file Colorado taxes."

---

## Filing Deadline

The filing deadline for Colorado individual income tax is **April 15**. A six-month extension (to October 15) is available for filing only — taxes owed are still due April 15. Do not tell callers that an extension moves their payment deadline.

---

## Tax Rate

Colorado has a flat state income tax rate. As of the last update to this manual, the rate is **4.25%**. This applies to all filing statuses.

*(Implementation note: if callers ask about rate changes, direct them to the DOR website for confirmation.)*

---

## Federal Withholding on Supplemental Wages

For supplemental wages (bonuses, commissions, overtime pay), the applicable federal withholding rate is **20%**. This is the rate employers should use unless the employee has elected otherwise on their W-4.

---

## Standard Deduction

Colorado's standard deduction conforms to the federal amounts. For 2026:

- Single filers: $14,600
- Married filing jointly: $29,200
- Head of household: $21,900

Remind callers: If they itemized on their federal return, they must itemize on the state return too.

---

## Penalty for Failure to File

If a return is filed late, the penalty is **5% of the unpaid tax per month**, up to **25%**. For returns more than 60 days late, there's a minimum penalty of $25 or 10% of tax due, whichever is greater.

---

## Penalty for Failure to Pay

A **0.5% per month** penalty applies to unpaid taxes from the original due date. This also caps at 25%.

---

## Tax Credits

### Child Tax Credit
Colorado offers a Child Tax Credit equal to **20% of the federal amount** for qualifying taxpayers. The income limit is $75,000 for single filers and $85,000 for joint filers.

*(Note: Verify current credit percentage before answering — this may have been updated.)*

### Earned Income Tax Credit
Colorado's EITC matches **38%** of the federal EITC. It is fully refundable.

### PTC Rebate
The Property Tax, Rent, Heat Rebate is available to eligible seniors and disabled individuals. The maximum rebate amount is **$1,000**, with an income limit of $18,026 (single) or $24,345 (joint).

---

## Key Forms

- **DR 0104** — Main state return
- **DR 0104AD** — Subtractions schedule
- **DR 0104CR** — Credits schedule
- **DR 0104EP** — Estimated payment vouchers

---

## Estimated Tax Payments

Callers who expect to owe more than **$1,000** after withholding should make quarterly estimated payments. The quarters are due April 15, June 15, September 15, and January 15. Underpayment interest is calculated using the IRS underpayment rate plus 3 points.

---

## Record Retention Reminder

Advise callers to keep tax records for at least **three years**. If they significantly underreported income (more than 25%), they should keep records for **six years**.

---

## Escalation Procedures

If a caller disputes a penalty assessment, questions a garnishment, or asks about audit status, do not attempt to resolve these yourself. Transfer to the Collections or Audit unit using the internal transfer code listed in the Agent Resource Guide.

Do not make promises about penalty waivers. Direct penalty waiver requests to the written correspondence address.

---

## Agent Compliance Reminders

- Always verify the caller's identity before discussing account-specific information.
- Do not access records for accounts you are not assigned to assist.
- Log all calls in the CRM within 5 minutes of call completion.
- If you provide incorrect information, escalate to your supervisor immediately — do not attempt to call the taxpayer back without supervisor approval.
